summary: The F30 improved on the E90's reliability with the refined N55 and later B48/B58 engines, but still carries typical BMW maintenance costs and some notable weak points.

issues.name: N55 Charge Pipe Failure
issues.description: The factory plastic charge pipe on the N55 engine cracks under boost pressure, causing sudden loss of power and a loud pop. A well-known weak point.

issues.name: Oil Filter Housing Gasket Leak
issues.description: The oil filter housing gasket is a chronic leak point on the N55. Oil drips onto the serpentine belt area and can cause further component damage.

issues.name: Electric Water Pump Failure
issues.description: The electric water pump can fail without warning, leading to overheating. Improved over the E90 generation but still a known failure point.

issues.name: Timing Chain Guide Wear (B48)
issues.description: Early B48 engines can develop timing chain stretch and guide wear, causing a rattling noise on cold start and eventual timing issues.

issues.name: Coolant Loss (Expansion Tank)
issues.description: The plastic coolant expansion tank can crack and leak, especially in cold climates. Gradual coolant loss may go unnoticed until overheating occurs.

buyingTips: B58 engine (340i) is the most reliable engine option — it addressed most N55 issues and is overbuilt
Check for the charge pipe recall on N55 models — a $20 aluminum replacement eliminates the issue permanently
Inspect under the car for oil drips from the oil filter housing area — a very common leak
Verify maintenance history — F30s with strict BMW maintenance intervals (every 15K miles) often need earlier oil changes
Test the iDrive system thoroughly — electronic gremlins with the infotainment are common on higher-mileage cars
